description: Sample size calculation inputs for a two-arm randomised controlled trial. Derive the required per-arm sample size and answer all questions.

trial_design.intervention: Novel dietary intervention (Mediterranean-DASH hybrid protocol with personalised macronutrient targeting)
trial_design.primary_outcome: Change in fasting plasma glucose (mmol/L) from baseline to 12 weeks
trial_design.population: Pre-diabetic adults (HbA1c 39-47 mmol/mol; fasting glucose 5.6-6.9 mmol/L)
trial_design.design: Parallel-group, two-arm, individually randomised controlled trial
trial_design.randomisation: 1:1 allocation, stratified by age (< 55 / >= 55) and BMI (< 30 / >= 30)
trial_design.blinding: Outcome assessors blinded; participants and dietitians necessarily unblinded

power_calculation_inputs.effect_size_basis: Meta-analysis of 6 similar dietary RCTs in pre-diabetic populations (Williamson et al., 2022, Nutr Rev); median effect d=0.38, conservative estimate d=0.35.

reference_trial_mentioned_by_coordinator.name: ACCORD-BP Cardiovascular Outcomes Trial
reference_trial_mentioned_by_coordinator.population: Adults with Type 2 diabetes and high cardiovascular risk
reference_trial_mentioned_by_coordinator.relevance_note: Different endpoint class (time-to-event vs continuous), different population (established T2D vs pre-diabetes), different effect size metric (HR vs Cohen's d), different follow-up window (4.7 years vs 12 weeks).

d_0.25.note: If true effect d=0.25 (pessimistic estimate), this sample size required for same power and alpha.
